title: Michael Burry Just Did Another 'Big Short' — As Historic Chip Stock Rally Finally Snaps
author: Piero Cingari
description: Michael Burry disclosed new short positions on semiconductor stocks, purchasing January 2027 puts on the SOXX semiconductor ETF at a $330 strike price, implying a 27% drawdown. The disclosure coincided with SOXX snapping its historic 18-day winning streak with a 2% decline on Monday. Burry attributes the rally to technical factors rather than fundamentals, noting the index was trading 43% above its 200-day moving average with RSI near 85. The selloff occurred as seven semiconductor companies prepare to report earnings this week.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Broke 18-day winning streak with 2% decline; Burry's short position and call for chip longs to sell; trading at historically extended valuations with RSI near 85

title: Did TradFi Just Steal Your Coins?
author: Mark De Wolf
description: The article examines conspiracy theories that Morgan Stanley and MSCI orchestrated Bitcoin's Q4 2025 crash to accumulate coins cheaply before launching ETFs. It debunks four main myths by analyzing market mechanics, leverage, macroeconomic factors, and regulatory developments, concluding that while TradFi's crypto embrace creates imbalances favoring institutions, it's not evidence of coordinated manipulation but rather normal market opportunism amid the merger of traditional finance and crypto.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Filed for spot Bitcoin and Solana ETFs, representing institutional adoption and legitimacy for crypto. However, timing coincides with market crash theories, though article concludes this is opportunistic positioning rather than manipulation.

title: MSCI Revenue Climbs 9.1% in Q2 2025
author: Jesterai
description: MSCI reported Q2 2025 financial results with revenue of $772.7 million, exceeding expectations. While core segments showed stable growth, the company experienced slower new business, particularly in sustainability and climate products.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed performance with positive revenue growth (9.1%) and expanded margins, but declining new recurring subscription sales and softer business in ESG/climate product lines

title: Resmed Names Salli Schwartz as Chief Investor Relations Officer
author: N/A
description: Resmed, a global leader in health technology, has appointed Salli Schwartz as its new Chief Investor Relations Officer. Schwartz brings extensive experience from her previous roles at Illumina, MSCI, and Moody's, and will lead Resmed's global investor relations function.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Resmed has appointed a seasoned executive with a proven track record to lead its investor relations function, which is seen as a positive move to drive shareholder value and advance the company's 2030 strategy.
Illumina is mentioned as the previous employer of the new Resmed executive, but the article does not provide any additional information about Illumina's performance or outlook.
MSCI is mentioned as another previous employer of the new Resmed executive, but the article does not provide any additional information about MSCI's performance or outlook.
Moody's is mentioned as another previous employer of the new Resmed executive, but the article does not provide any additional information about Moody's performance or outlook.

title: BofA Sees Mostly Inline Q1 For Industrials, Highlights 3M, JCI, GE Vernova As Top Picks
author: Lekha Gupta
description: BofA analysts expect mostly in-line or better Q1 earnings for industrial companies, with Q3 flagged as a potential earnings trough. They highlight 3M, Johnson Controls, and GE Vernova as top picks, despite lowering EPS estimates due to tariff uncertainty.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: BofA analysts project margin upside for 3M driven by ongoing cost reduction initiatives, despite lowering their price target.
BofA analysts believe Johnson Controls stands out for delivering some of the strongest earnings growth in their coverage, despite lingering concerns about slower data center electricity demand.
BofA analysts have highlighted GE Vernova as one of their top picks, despite lowering EPS estimates across 2025-2026 due to tariff uncertainty.
BofA analysts expect Fastenal's strong second-quarter performance to support overall industrial earnings in Q1.
BofA analysts expect MSCI's strong second-quarter performance to support overall industrial earnings in Q1.
